description: Run the last command.

name: history
description: List command history with line numbers.

name: history N
description: List the last N commands from history.

name: pwd

name: ls
description: List directory contents.

description: List all files including hidden ones.

name: ls -la
description: List all files including hidden ones in long form.

name: ls -l
description: List directory contents in long form.

name: ls -lh

name: ls -t

name: stat

name: tree

name: tree -d

name: cd
description: Go to home directory (shorthand for cd ~).

name: cd ~
description: Go to home directory.

name: cd -
description: Go to last directory.

name: pushd
description: Go to a sub-directory and push the previous directory onto the stack.

name: popd
description: Go back to the directory saved by pushd.

name: mkdir
description: Create a directory.

name: mkdir -p
description: Create nested directories.

name: mktemp -d
description: Create a temporary directory.

name: cp -R
description: Copy a directory recursively.

name: rsync -zv
description: Copy directory, overwrites destination.

name: rsync -azv
description: Copy directory without overwriting destination.

name: rsync local→remote
description: Copy local directory to remote directory.

name: rsync remote→local
description: Copy remote directory to local directory.

name: rmdir
description: Delete an empty directory.

name: rm -r
description: Delete a directory and its contents.

name: rm -rf
description: Delete a directory and its contents, ignoring nonexistent files and never prompting.

name: touch
description: Create a file or update its modified timestamp.

name: mktemp
description: Create a temporary file.

name: cp
description: Copy a file.

name: mv
description: Move or rename a file.

name: rm
description: Delete a file.

name: rm -f
description: Delete a file, ignoring nonexistent files and never prompting.

name: cat
description: Print all file contents.

name: less
description: Print file contents one page at a time (g: top, SHIFT+g: bottom, /foo: search).

name: head
description: Print the top 10 lines of a file.
notes:
Use `-n` to specify the number of lines:

```
head -n 50 file    # print the top 50 lines
```

name: tail
description: Print the bottom 10 lines of a file.
notes:
Use `-n` to specify the number of lines:

```
tail -n 50 file    # print the bottom 50 lines
```

`-f` follows the file in real time, useful for watching logs:

```
tail -f file
```

name: open
description: Open a file in its default application.

name: wc
description: Count lines, words and characters in a file.
notes:
### Flags

| Flag | Counts |
|------|--------|
| `-l` | Lines |
| `-w` | Words |
| `-c` | Bytes |
| `-m` | Characters |

Without flags, `wc` prints all three (lines, words, bytes) at once.

name: ln -s
description: Create a symbolic link.

name: ln -sf
description: Overwrite an existing symbolic link.

name: sed replace
description: Replace a pattern in a file and output to console.
notes:
### Expression breakdown

The expression follows the pattern `s/pattern/replacement/flags`:

| Part | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `s` | Substitute |
| `pattern` | What to find (supports regex) |
| `replacement` | What to replace it with |
| `g` | Global — replace all occurrences, not just the first |
| `i` | Case-insensitive match |

```
sed 's/foo/bar/'     # replaces first match per line
sed 's/foo/bar/g'    # replaces all matches
sed 's/foo/bar/gi'   # all matches, case-insensitive
```

name: sed replace -i
description: Replace a pattern in a file and overwrite it in place.
notes:
### Expression breakdown

The expression follows the pattern `s/pattern/replacement/flags`:

| Part | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `s` | Substitute |
| `pattern` | What to find (supports regex) |
| `replacement` | What to replace it with |
| `g` | Global — replace all occurrences, not just the first |
| `i` | Case-insensitive match |

`-i` overwrites the file in place. Without it, output is printed to the console only.

name: sed replace -i case
description: Replace a pattern case-insensitively in a file and output to console.
notes:
### Expression breakdown

The expression follows the pattern `s/pattern/replacement/flags`:

| Part | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `s` | Substitute |
| `pattern` | What to find (supports regex) |
| `replacement` | What to replace it with |
| `g` | Global — replace all occurrences, not just the first |
| `i` | Case-insensitive match |

name: awk
description: Extract and process fields from structured text.
notes:
### Column extraction

`$1`, `$2`, etc. refer to columns separated by whitespace. `$0` is the whole line:

```
awk '{print $1}' file          # first column
awk '{print $1, $3}' file      # first and third columns
awk -F: '{print $1}' /etc/passwd  # use : as delimiter
```

### Filtering rows

```
awk '/error/ {print $0}' file      # lines matching 'error'
awk '$3 > 100 {print}' file        # lines where column 3 > 100
```

name: sort
description: Sort lines of a file alphabetically.

name: sort -r
description: Sort lines in reverse order.

name: sort -n
description: Sort lines numerically.

name: uniq
description: Remove consecutive duplicate lines. Pipe from sort to remove all duplicates.

name: sort | uniq
description: Remove all duplicate lines (sort first to group duplicates together).

name: cut
description: Extract a specific column from a delimited file.
notes:
### Flags

| Flag | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `-d` | Delimiter character |
| `-f` | Field number(s) to extract |

```
cut -d ':' -f 1 /etc/passwd     # extract usernames
cut -d ',' -f 1,3 file.csv      # extract columns 1 and 3
cut -d ' ' -f 2- file           # extract from column 2 onwards
```

name: diff
description: Show differences between two files.

name: diff -u
description: Show differences in unified format (the format used in patches and PRs).

name: tee
description: Write command output to both a file and stdout simultaneously.

name: chmod +x
description: Give everybody execute permission.
notes:
### Symbolic — `chmod [who][operator][permission] <file>`

**Who**

| Symbol | Target |
|--------|--------|
| `u` | User (owner) |
| `g` | Group |
| `o` | Others |
| `a` | All of the above |

**Operator**

| Symbol | Effect |
|--------|--------|
| `+` | Add permission |
| `-` | Remove permission |
| `=` | Set exactly (replaces existing) |

**Permission**

| Symbol | Meaning |
|--------|--------|
| `r` | Read |
| `w` | Write |
| `x` | Execute (or enter, for directories) |

**Examples**

```
chmod u+x file       # give user execute
chmod g+rwx file     # give group all permissions
chmod o-w file       # remove write from others
chmod a=r file       # set everyone to read-only
```

---

### Numeric — `chmod [user][group][others] <file>`

Each digit (0–7) represents the combined permissions for that target. You always provide all three.

| # | Permissions | rwx |
|---|-------------|-----|
| 7 | read + write + execute | rwx |
| 6 | read + write | rw- |
| 5 | read + execute | r-x |
| 4 | read only | r-- |
| 3 | write + execute | -wx |
| 2 | write only | -w- |
| 1 | execute only | --x |
| 0 | none | --- |

**Examples**

```
chmod 755 file   # user: rwx, group: r-x, others: r-x
chmod 644 file   # user: rw-, group: r--, others: r--
chmod 700 file   # user: rwx, group: ---, others: ---
```

**Common defaults**

| Value | Result | Use case |
|-------|--------|----------|
| `644` | rw-r--r-- | Default file |
| `755` | rwxr-xr-x | Default directory |

name: chmod u+x
description: Give the user execute permission.
notes:
### Symbolic — `chmod [who][operator][permission] <file>`

**Who:** `u` = user, `g` = group, `o` = others, `a` = all

**Operator:** `+` add, `-` remove, `=` set exactly

**Permission:** `r` read, `w` write, `x` execute

```
chmod u+x file     # add execute for user
chmod g+rwx file   # add all for group
chmod o-w file     # remove write for others
```

---

### Numeric — `chmod [user][group][others] <file>`

| # | rwx | Permissions |
|---|-----|-------------|
| 7 | rwx | read + write + execute |
| 6 | rw- | read + write |
| 5 | r-x | read + execute |
| 4 | r-- | read only |
| 0 | --- | none |

```
chmod 755 file   # rwxr-xr-x
chmod 644 file   # rw-r--r--
```

name: chmod g+x
description: Give the group execute permission.
notes:
### Symbolic — `chmod [who][operator][permission] <file>`

**Who:** `u` = user, `g` = group, `o` = others, `a` = all

**Operator:** `+` add, `-` remove, `=` set exactly

**Permission:** `r` read, `w` write, `x` execute

```
chmod g+x file     # add execute for group
chmod g+rwx file   # add all permissions for group
chmod g-w file     # remove write for group
```

---

### Numeric — `chmod [user][group][others] <file>`

| # | rwx | Permissions |
|---|-----|-------------|
| 7 | rwx | read + write + execute |
| 6 | rw- | read + write |
| 5 | r-x | read + execute |
| 4 | r-- | read only |
| 0 | --- | none |

```
chmod 755 file   # user: rwx, group: r-x, others: r-x
chmod 070 file   # user: ---, group: rwx, others: ---
```

name: chmod u-x,g-x
description: Take away execute permission from user and group.
notes:
### Symbolic — `chmod [who][operator][permission] <file>`

You can chain multiple targets in one command by separating them with a comma:

```
chmod u-x,g-x file     # remove execute from both user and group
chmod u+rw,o-rwx file  # add rw to user, remove all from others
```

**Who:** `u` = user, `g` = group, `o` = others, `a` = all

**Operator:** `+` add, `-` remove, `=` set exactly

**Permission:** `r` read, `w` write, `x` execute

name: chmod a+x
description: Give all users execute permission.
notes:
### Symbolic — `chmod [who][operator][permission] <file>`

`a` targets all three groups (user, group, others) at once. It is equivalent to writing `u+x,g+x,o+x`.

```
chmod a+x file   # give everyone execute
chmod a+r file   # give everyone read
chmod a-w file   # remove write from everyone
chmod a=r file   # set everyone to read-only (replaces existing)
```

**Who:** `u` = user, `g` = group, `o` = others, `a` = all

**Operator:** `+` add, `-` remove, `=` set exactly

**Permission:** `r` read, `w` write, `x` execute

name: chmod +100
description: Add 1 to the user permission.
notes:
## Numeric chmod

The three-digit form sets permissions for **user**, **group**, and **others** in order. Each digit is 0–7.

| # | rwx | Permissions |
|---|-----|-------------|
| 7 | rwx | read + write + execute |
| 6 | rw- | read + write |
| 5 | r-x | read + execute |
| 4 | r-- | read only |
| 3 | -wx | write + execute |
| 2 | -w- | write only |
| 1 | --x | execute only |
| 0 | --- | none |

```
chmod 644 file   # user: rw-, group: r--, others: r--
chmod 755 file   # user: rwx, group: r-x, others: r-x
chmod 700 file   # user: rwx, group: ---, others: ---
```

`chmod +100` adds `1` (execute) to the user digit without touching group or others.

name: chmod -100
description: Subtract 1 from the user permission.
notes:
## Numeric chmod

The three-digit form sets permissions for **user**, **group**, and **others** in order. Each digit is 0–7.

| # | rwx | Permissions |
|---|-----|-------------|
| 7 | rwx | read + write + execute |
| 6 | rw- | read + write |
| 5 | r-x | read + execute |
| 4 | r-- | read only |
| 3 | -wx | write + execute |
| 2 | -w- | write only |
| 1 | --x | execute only |
| 0 | --- | none |

`chmod -100` removes `1` (execute) from the user digit without touching group or others.

name: chown
description: Change file owner and group.

name: type
description: Find the binary for a command.

name: which
description: Find the binary path for a command.

name: whereis
description: Find the binary, source, and manual page files for a command.

name: updatedb
description: Update the locate database index.

name: locate
description: Find a file using the index (fast).

name: locate -i
description: Find a file using the index, ignoring case.

name: find -name
description: Find a file by name.
notes:
### Running commands on results

Append `-exec` to run a command on each matched file:

```
find . -name "*.log" -exec rm {} \;
find . -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \;
```

See `find -exec` for the full reference.

name: find -iname
description: Find a file by name, case insensitive.
notes:
### Running commands on results

Append `-exec` to run a command on each matched file:

```
find . -iname "*.Log" -exec rm {} \;
```

See `find -exec` for the full reference.

name: find -name *.ext
description: Find all files with a given extension.
notes:
### Running commands on results

Append `-exec` to run a command on each matched file:

```
find . -name "*.png" -exec pngquant {} \;
find . -name "*.log" -exec rm {} +
```

See `find -exec` for the full reference.

name: find -delete
description: Find a file and delete it.

name: find -type f
description: Find a regular file.

name: find -type d
description: Find a directory.

name: find -type l
description: Find a symbolic link.

name: find -mtime
description: Find files that haven't been modified in 30 days.

name: find -mtime -delete
description: Delete files that haven't been modified in 30 days.

name: find -exec
description: Run a command on each file found.
notes:
### The -exec flag

`{}` is a placeholder for the matched file. `\;` terminates the command.

```
find . -name "*.log" -exec rm {} \;
find . -type f -exec chmod 644 {} \;
```

Use `+` instead of `\;` to pass all results at once — faster for large sets:

```
find . -name "*.log" -exec rm {} +
```

name: grep
description: Search for a pattern in a file.
notes:
### Extended regex with `-E`

`-E` enables extended regex, allowing the use of the following operators without backslashes:

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `\|` | OR — match either side | `error\|warning` |
| `+` | One or more of the preceding char | `fo+` matches `fo`, `foo` |
| `?` | Zero or one of the preceding char | `https?` matches `http`, `https` |
| `()` | Group expressions | `(foo\|bar)` |

name: grep -r
description: Recursively search for a pattern in a directory.

name: grep -R
description: Recursively search for a pattern and follow symbolic links.

name: grep -l
description: Show only files that match the pattern.

name: grep -L
description: Show only files that don't match the pattern.

name: grep -i
description: Case insensitive pattern search.

name: grep -x
description: Match the entire line.

name: grep -C
description: Show N lines of context above and below each match.

name: grep -v
description: Show only lines that don't match the pattern.

name: grep -c
description: Count the number of matching lines.

name: grep -n
description: Show line numbers alongside results.

name: grep -E
description: Search using extended regular expressions.
notes:
### Extended regex with `-E`

`-E` enables extended regex, allowing the use of the following operators without backslashes:

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `\|` | OR — match either side | `error\|warning` |
| `+` | One or more of the preceding char | `fo+` matches `fo`, `foo` |
| `?` | Zero or one of the preceding char | `https?` matches `http`, `https` |
| `()` | Group expressions | `(foo\|bar)` |

name: egrep
description: Search using extended regular expressions (alias for grep -E).
notes:
### Extended regex with `-E`

`-E` enables extended regex, allowing the use of the following operators without backslashes:

| Operator | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|---------|
| `\|` | OR — match either side | `error\|warning` |
| `+` | One or more of the preceding char | `fo+` matches `fo`, `foo` |
| `?` | Zero or one of the preceding char | `https?` matches `http`, `https` |
| `()` | Group expressions | `(foo\|bar)` |

name: xargs
description: Build and execute commands from standard input.
notes:
### Usage

`xargs` reads items from stdin and passes them as arguments to a command. Commonly paired with `find` or `grep`:

```
find . -name "*.log" | xargs rm
grep -rl 'foo' . | xargs sed -i 's/foo/bar/g'
```

Use `-I {}` to control where the argument is placed:

```
find . -name "*.txt" | xargs -I {} cp {} /backup/
```

name: zip
description: Compress a file into a .zip archive.

name: zip -r
description: Compress a directory into a .zip archive.

name: unzip
description: Extract a .zip archive into the current directory.

name: gzip
description: Compress <file> into <file>.gz and delete the original.

name: gzip -k
description: Compress a file into a .gz archive and keep the original.

name: gzip -d
description: Decompress <file>.gz and restore the original file.

name: gunzip
description: Decompress a .gz archive and delete it.

name: gunzip -k
description: Decompress a .gz archive and keep the original.

name: tar -czf
description: Compress a directory into a .tgz archive.
notes:
### Flag breakdown

Flags are combined into one block — no spaces needed:

| Flag | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `c` | Create archive |
| `x` | Extract archive |
| `z` | Compress/decompress with gzip (.tar.gz / .tgz) |
| `f` | Next argument is the filename |
| `v` | Verbose — list files as they are processed |

```
tar -czf archive.tgz dir/    # create
tar -xzf archive.tgz         # extract
tar -xzf archive.tgz -v      # extract with file list
```

name: tar -xzf
description: Extract a .tar.gz archive into the current directory.
notes:
### Flag breakdown

| Flag | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `c` | Create archive |
| `x` | Extract archive |
| `z` | Compress/decompress with gzip (.tar.gz / .tgz) |
| `f` | Next argument is the filename |
| `v` | Verbose — list files as they are processed |

```
tar -xzf archive.tgz         # extract
tar -xzf archive.tgz -v      # extract with file list
tar -xzf archive.tgz -C dir/ # extract into a specific directory
```

name: tar -xf
description: Extract a .tar archive into the current directory.
notes:
### Flag breakdown

| Flag | Meaning |
|------|---------|
| `c` | Create archive |
| `x` | Extract archive |
| `z` | Compress/decompress with gzip (.tar.gz / .tgz) |
| `f` | Next argument is the filename |
| `v` | Verbose — list files as they are processed |

No `z` flag here — `.tar` files are not gzip-compressed, just bundled.

description: Start a new screen session (CTRL-a + d to detach).

name: screen -S
description: Start a new named screen session.

name: screen -ls
description: List all screen sessions.

name: screen -R
description: Reattach to a screen session by name or ID.

name: screen -d
description: Detach a screen session that is running in another terminal.

name: exit
description: Exit the current shell session.
Redirect stdout to a file, overwriting it.
Redirect stdout to a file, appending to it.
Redirect stderr to a file.
Redirect both stdout and stderr to a file.
notes:
### File descriptors

| # | Stream |
|---|--------|
| `1` | stdout (standard output) |
| `2` | stderr (standard error) |

`2>&1` means: send stderr (`2`) to wherever stdout (`1`) is going.

name: /dev/null
description: Discard all output. Use > /dev/null for stdout only, 2> /dev/null for stderr only.
Pipe stdout of one command as stdin to the next.

name: alias
description: Create a shortcut for a command.

name: alias list
description: List all currently defined aliases.

name: unalias
description: Remove an alias.
